def: adj. & s.m. Giving milk, granting or yielding desirable objects; — a milkman, milker.
s.m. = next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī) = dohī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Giving milk, granting or yielding desirable objects; — a milkman, milker.
s.m. Milker, milkman ( = dohī).
n. m. One who milks cattle.

s.f. Service-lands; rent-free lands granted to Brāhmans or to religious mendicants for a public or charitable object (as the sinking of wells, the erection of an assembly-room, the building and maintenance of a temple, &c.).

n. f. Rent-free lands granted to religious mendicants or brahmans for a public or charitable object, as the sinking of a well, the erection of a chaupā/?/ , the building and maintenance of a temple, etc.

v. a. S. दुह Pālī. dohatī . To milk. Pahle dahī jamāë-ke pāchhe duhiye g/?/ë, Bachchā uske peṭ meṅ dahī bikāan jāe . Riddle. Ans. A fim opium. The tyre first set, then milked should be the cow, The calf formed in the womb, the tyre sold now. dohā'o , n. m. The zamindar's perquisite of a certain quantity of milk from the cultivator's cows.

ety: doh˚ = S. दोहय(ति), caus. fr. rt. दुह्
def: v.t. To milk; — s.m. Giving or yielding milk or other desirable objects; anything that yields milk, &c. ( = dohan); — a milk-pail
n. f. dohnā , n. m. A milk-pail.
n. f. Oil; ointment.

ety: ḍoh, q.v.+rā = Prk. अडओ = S र+कः
def: s.m. A large ladle or spoon (used in sugar-manufactories, &c.).

adj. Twice; double; two folds. dohrā ārā dantī , ( Bot .) Serrate. dohrā parīlā , ( Bot .) Bipinnate. dohrā tehrā , Two to three; heaps. dohrā tīyā , ( Bot .) Biternate. dohrā datīlā , Double-toothed. dohrā-shabd ; A. murakkab , S. यौगिक ( Gram .) A compound word. dohrī gāt , adj. Plump; fat; in good case. dohrā gol-kaṭā , ( Bot .) Doubly crenate. dohrā ho jānā , v. n. To be doubled up or bent. dohrī , ( Bot .) Induplicate. dohrī-lāg ; A. tanāsub-i-arbā ( Arith .) Proportion.
more: n. m. A wooden spoon with which the juice of sugar-cane is stirred and ladled out.

ety: Ap. Prk. दोहडउ, Prk. दुविहडओ; S. द्वि+विध+र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Double, two-fold, twice as much; reduplicated; compound; — s.m. A couplet, distich ( = dohā, q.v.)
more: s. m. A distich. adj. Double.
subs: v.n. To be doubled, be folded up, be curved or bent.

ety: dohrā(nā)+Prk. एअव्वं+अं and अइआ; S. (इ)तव्यं+कं and ता+इका
def: s.m. An equivoque, a double-entendre (syn. ćhekoktī).
v. n. 1. To repeat; say or do again; to take a retrospect. 2. To revise; go over again.

ety: dohrā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To fold, to double; to reduplicate; to repeat (a word, or thing, or an act); to revise (a lesson, &c.); — v.n. To return (a sickness or attack); to suffer a relapse.
more: v. a. To repeat: see دهرانا duhrānā.

ety: dohrā(nā)+Prk. एअव्वं+अं and अइआ; S. (इ)तव्यं+कं and ता+इका
def: s.m. An equivoque, a double-entendre (syn. ćhekoktī).
more: (from دهرانا duhrānā, q. v.) s. m. Repetition, doubling, reduplication.

ety: dohrā(nā)+Prk. एअव्वं+अं and अइआ; S. (इ)तव्यं+कं and ता+इका
def: s.m. An equivoque, a double-entendre (syn. ćhekoktī).

s.f. = dohlī, q.v. s.f. Service-lands; rent-free lands granted to Brāhmans or to religious mendicants for a public or charitable object (as the sinking of wells, the erection of an assembly-room, the building and maintenance of a temple, &c.).
s.m. Daughter's son, grandson.
more: s. m. Daughterʼs son. See دوهتر.

def: s.f. Daughter's daughter, granddaughter.
(cf. next), s.m.(?), A sandy sub-soil.
s.f. A (wooden) spoon, a ladle; — an oar, paddle; — head, poll, pate
subs: s.f. 'Breaking the head'; wearisome head-work (as that of instructing a blockhead, &c.); any toilsome and vain exertion
s.m. A class of mendicants who are so importunate that rather than depart empty-handed they will beat their heads against stones; an individual of this class; (fig.) an importunate petitioner.

def: s. f. A wooden spoon, ladle.
n. m. A class of Hin. mendicants who knock their heads against stones to enforce compliance with their demands. Hence, any importunate petitioner.
adj. Married to a second wife; — s.m. = dūj-bar, q.v. s.m. A man who marries a second wife, a bigamist (cf. dojhā).
more: adj. Married to a second wife.

def: adj. (dakh.) Two-tongued, having two tongues, double-tongued ( Harris ).
s.m. A two-year-old colt.
more: n. f. Pulmonary disease in animals.
s. m. A two year old foal.

def: s.f. Vomiting; — pulmonary disease in animals; — a loud or large gulp; a large draught (cf. ḍhok; syn. ghūṅṭ).
s.m. = dosh, 'defect,' q.v. adv. Yesterday evening, last night.

n. m. Blame. See دوش Bālī thī jab khūb thī, nā jānūṅ kuchh aur, Chaṛhī javānī phūṅk dūṅ! jahāṅ khaṛī vahīṅ dokh! In childhood I was happy, then I nothing knew, Burn puberty! whate'er I do I'm blamed for you!
more: s. m. A crime, a fault, blame, vice, blemish. See دوس dos.
s. (dakh.) Pain, grief: see دکهـ dukh.

def: adj. & s.m. corr. of doshak, q.v.
s. Objection, blame.
s.f. A raised mound indicating the junction of two boundaries.

adj. & s.m. corr. of doshī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Faulty, defective; corrupt, vitiated, contaminated, defiled; blameable, culpable, bad, wicked, guilty, criminal; — a wicked or a guilty person, a sinner, &c.
more: n. m. An offender; a criminal.
n. A raised mound, indicating the junction of two boundaries ( had-bast ).

def: v.t. = doshnā, q.v. v.t. To find fault with, to censure, withstand, blame, reproach, accuse; to calumniate.
more: v. a. See دوشنا Āp kare, mohe dokh lagāve, aiso suāmī nāh suhāve . Hin. Wom.
v. a. To accuse, to calumniate, to blame.

ety: v.n. of doḵẖtan; rt. Zend dug = दुह् 'to milk,' &c.
def: s.f. Sewing; a stitch; a seam
more: (from دوختن) s. f. Sewing, a stitch, seam. دوخت کرنا doḵẖt karnā, To sew.

ety: perf. part. of doḵẖtan; see doḵẖt
def: part. adj. Sewed, stitched; fixed (as the eye or sight), transfixed.
s.f. The act of vomiting.
v.n. To vomit
subs: to take large gulps or draughts
to drink much, guzzle, tope ( = ḍukḍukānā, and ḍhoknā).

def: v. n. To drink in large draughts; quaff; guzzle; imbibe.
adj. & s.m. (f. ī), Old, aged, decrepit (gen. used contemptuously or abusively); — an old man; — ḍokrī, s.f. An old woman.
n. m. An old man.
n. f. An old woman ( buṛhyā ).
adj. f. Old (woman).

ety: said to be fr. A. دلو dalw, by transposition; prob. connected with S. dola
def: s.m. A bucket ( = ḍol, q.v.); — the mast of a ship; a bag, a purse (cf. dulmiyan).

s.m. A bucket (of metal, or leather, for drawing water), a pail, a large leather bag (used in throwing up water for irrigation); — (local) mast of a ship; — the richest kind of black soil (cf daldal).
s.m. Moving, swinging, rocking, &c. (see ḍolnā)
more: (Pers. دول) s. m. A bucket for drawing water.
subs: s.m. Moving or swaying from side to side or to and fro, restlessness, unsettled state
s.m. Moving to and fro, roving, perambulating.

def: s.m. Swinging, oscillating; a swing, a swinging cot; a litter, a ḍūlī; a festival on the fourteenth of the month Phāgun (Feb.-March), when figures of the juvenile Kr̤ishṇa are swung in an ornamental swing
more: n. m. S. दुल to raise up; P. dalva a bucket. A metal or leathern bucket used for drawing water from a well, or for throwing up water for irrigation. Man. ' Kāhe ko thāro māṭkā, aur kāhe ko thāro ḍol? Kāhe kī thārī īṅḍvī, gorī, aur kahā hai thāro mol? ' Wom. ' Sone kā mhārā māṭ kā, aur rūpe kā mhārā ḍol, Ratan jaṛāū kī īṅḍvī, lākh ṭake mhārā mol! ' Man. ' Māṭī kā thārā māṭkā, aur chamṛe kā thārā ḍol, Ghās phūṅs kī īṅḍvī, gorī, do kauṛī thārā mol! ' Sonā and Gorī . 'What is your pitcher made of, what your bucket made? Your pitcher's cushion what, and what's your price, fair maid?' 'My bucket is of silver, and my pitcher's gold, My cushion's set with gems, my price one lakh full told.' 'Your pitcher's earthen, and your bucket leather just, Your cushion's grass and straw, two cowries worth you're just.'
subs: v.n. To swing, to wave; to undulate; to hesitate
vulg. ḍol-jātrā, s.f. corr. of dolā-yātrā, q.v.s.v. dolā.

ety: Prk. डोलओ, or दोलओ; S. दोलकः
def: s.m. A swing; a cradle; a litter; a kind of sedan (in which women of rank are carried); a 'dooley' ( = ḍolī; — (local) a bier; — a woman of inferior rank given to a person of rank and position (to whom she is privately married without any ceremonial: she ranks below wives of equal family, but above concubines)
more: s. m. 1. A kind of sedan. 2. A wife from an inferiour family, married by a person of rank, who gives a present to her parents. She ranks below wives of equal family, but above concubines. ڐولا دينا ḍolā-denā, To give a daughter to a superiour by way of tribute.
subs: (-ko), To give a daughter to a superior by way of tribute.

def: s. m. 1. Manner. 2. Coition. (A phrase).
s.f. A swing, &c. ( = ḍolā, q.v.); — fluctuation, incertitude
more: n. m. S. दोला 1. A kind of cradle swung from a tree, etc. 2. A kind of sedan in which women of rank are carried. 3. A dooley. 4. A woman of inferior rank presented to the paramount Chief to whom she is privately married without any ceremonial. ḍolā denā , v. n. To give a daughter to a superior by way of tribute.
subs: s.f. 'The swing-festival,' name of a festival in honour of Kr̤ishṇa, when figures of him and his consort Rādhā are carried about in a litter or swung in an ornamental swing.

r: dol-āb, daulāb, dūlāb, dol, āb, daul
ety: A. daulāb, dūlāb; fr. P. dol+āb; or A. daul, 'turning round'+āb
def: s.m. A vertical wheel with a series of earthen pots strung round it, with which water is raised for irrigation, a water-wheel, the Persian wheel; (met.) machination, artifice, trick, fraud.
more: (دول Bucket, and آب Water) s. m. A wheel for drawing water with. (Adopted in Arabic, and the Arab. plur. is دواليب).

def: adj. Resembling a water-wheel in its rotation, rotating, revolving; tricky, deceitful, treacherous.
v.t. = ḍulānā, q.v. v.t. To shake, agitate; to toss.
(see ڐ.لنا) s. (dakh.) Swinger, mover.
s. m. (dakh.) A sort of swing.
adj. Swinging, oscillating, rocking, being swung backwards and forwards; vacillating, wavering, doubting, perplexed.
s.m. A leathern bucket ( = ḍol-ćī).
s.m. = H ڐولچي डोलची ḍolćī, s.f. ( = ḍol-ća, q.v.), A small bucket (for drawing water), a pail.
n. f. A small bucket; a vessel for drawing water.
s.f. = H ڐولچا डोलचा ḍolćā, s.m. ( = ḍol-ća, q.v.), A small bucket (for drawing water), a pail.
more: (for دولچي) s. f. A small bucket for drawing water.

def: s. m. Proving, perambulating.

ety: Prk. डोलिआ, or दोलिआ; S. दोलिका
def: s.f. A litter, a kind of sedan (for women); — a small portable cot, a 'dooley.'
more: s. f. A kind of sedan (for women).

def: n. f. S. दौलिक a litter, Pr. ḍolā . See ḍolā , 2. 1. Pher de ḍulyā kahār, bhaujī, gaune na jaībā, Gaune ke laṛvā sab koī khaīyeṅ, jīrā jaihe hamār . Song. The doolie , sister-in-law, send back, to my spouse I will not go, The parting feast all will enjoy, my heart is breaking though! 2. Lālī lālī ḍoliyā līye ṭhāṛe, saṅg parohit aur nāī . Song. A litter by four bearers borne, the priest and barber both attend! (A bier). 3. Lāli lāli ḍoliyā phaṅdāo morā mītavā! Chalī jāuṅ naiharvā, Chait kaise kheloṅ! E. Song. Chait. A bright red gay sedan, dear friend, soon ready get for me! To my father's house I'll go, or how enjoy Chait's mirth and glee!
s.f. A swing ( = dolā).
more: s. f. A bastard son or daughter by illegitimate connexion with a widow.

def: n. m. A kahār or bearer caste who fetch water and carry doolies or palanquins.
v. a. To wave, to swing, to undulate, to hesitate.

ety: ḍol˚ = Prk. डोल(इ), or डोले(इ), or दोले(इ) = S. दोलय(ति), rt. दुल्;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To swing, oscillate, rock, roll, reel, sway from side to side; to shake, move, stir; to totter, tremble, quake; to flinch, swerve, waver; to move to and fro, to walk about, roam, rove, wander, ramble.
more: v. n. 1. To move, to shake or be shaken. 2. To roam, ramble, rove, wander. 3. To swing.

def: v. n. S. दुल to swing. 1. To swing; wave; oscillate; see-saw. 1. Chauthā pahrā din bhojan hoë taiyār, Āo, piu, tū jīm le! to ko aṅchrā ḍoloṅ bāo . Dohā. It is the fourth watch and your dinner's cooked for you, Come, love, and dine! my mantle shall soft breezes woo. 2. Tiryā tan kī pātlī, sīs ūjle kes, Ḍole pī ke sīs pe, pūjeṅ Gor Ganesh . Riddle. Ans. Chaurī . A body lank and lean, a head of fair white hair She waves her love's head on, Ganesh she worships e'er. 2. To move up and down, to and fro, backwards and forwards. 3. To wander; roam; ramble; rove. 1. Abhī bonī nahīṅ karī, tū jarā ḍol-ke ā jā . Chaubolā. 2. Āp kār bin ṭhālī ḍole, sīkh det to ko, balmā! Rus. Wom. song. Why idly wander you about—I do advise you husband mine. 4. To tremble; shake; to be all in a tremor. Bhūk lagī, tan ḍulne lāgā, mātā ṭūk nahīṅ detī, Ek pal kī bhī chhūṭī man ne, mūlan bhūk nahīṅ detī! Rus. song. With hunger shakes my frame, my mother wont give me A bit of bread, no moment from this hunger free. 5. To waver; swerve or go back from; to flinch; deviate. Wahāṅ se to kaul bachan kar āyā, yahāṅ tū ḍole naṭā naṭā . Hymn. In your former birth you promised fair, your faith forsworn you wander here.

s.m. A low caste of Hindūs (they make ropes, mats, baskets, fans, &c. &c., and are employed in removing carcases, filth, &c., and about burial and burning grounds: they are also tumblers and merry-andrews; and withal are great thieves: thus it will be seen that, besides the name, they have much in common with the gipsies or 'Rom'); — a caste of Mohammedans (perhaps converts from the Hindū ḍoms), the males of which are musicians, and their women singers, dancers and actresses (but they sing and dance &c. in the presence of women only); — an individual of the ḍom caste.
more: s. m. 1. A low cast of Hindūs. 2. The name of a cast of Musalmāns, the males of which are musicians, and the females sing and dance in the company of females only.

def: s. m. The art or practice of a male or female of the ڐوم ḍom cast.

ety: ḍoman, prob. dialec. plur. of ḍom
def: s.m. = ḍom-panā, q.v. s.m. The arts or practices of the ḍoms; ḍom-dom; — adulation.

s.m. A low caste of Hindūs (they make ropes, mats, baskets, fans, &c. &c., and are employed in removing carcases, filth, &c., and about burial and burning grounds: they are also tumblers and merry-andrews; and withal are great thieves: thus it will be seen that, besides the name, they have much in common with the gipsies or 'Rom'); — a caste of Mohammedans (perhaps converts from the Hindū ḍoms), the males of which are musicians, and their women singers, dancers and actresses (but they sing and dance &c. in the presence of women only); — an individual of the ḍom caste.
s.f. A woman or girl of the ḍom caste; the wife of a ḍom.
more: s. f. A female of the cast called ڐوم ḍom, above described.

def: s. f. plur. Females who only sing in presence of women, and play, sitting, on the ḍholak and manjīrā (Qanoon-e-islam).

def: s.m. The arts or practices of the ḍoms; ḍom-dom; — adulation.

def: s.m. The arts or practices of the ḍoms; ḍom-dom; — adulation.

def: 1. adj. Having two mouths. 2. s. m. Name of a serpent which has two mouths. (Amphisbæna).
s.m. A fractional division of an estate.
s.m. Leaves folded up in the shape of a cup (for holding flowers, sweetmeats, &c.), a basket or bucket made of large leaves.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa दूण (dūṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀉𑀡 (duuṇa), 𑀤𑀺𑀉𑀡 (diuṇa), from Sanskrit द्विगुण (dviguṇa).

subs: s.m. The Artemisia vulgaris; a kind of sweet marjoram.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa दूण (dūṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀉𑀡 (duuṇa), 𑀤𑀺𑀉𑀡 (diuṇa), from Sanskrit द्विगुण (dviguṇa).

def: adj. Double-barrelled (a gun).
adj. Two-barrelled (a gun). See دونالي do-nāl-ī.
s.f. A small kettle-drum (beaten by a public crier); proclamation by beat of drum.
more: s. f. Proclamation by beat of drum. See ڐهنڐهورا ḍhanḍhorā.

def: n. f. S. डिण्डिम 1. A small drum beaten by a public crier. 2. Proclamation by beat of drum. ḍauṅḍī phernā, yā pīṭnā , v. a. See ḍugḍugī pīṭnā .

s.m. A trough; a canoe, boat; any boat-shaped vessel; a ladle (commonly a cocoanut-shell with a long wooden handle), a large spoon; (in Anglo-Indian households) a double dish, hot water dish; a tureen; an entrée.

s.f. Branch (of a tree).
s.f. A small boat; — a ladle, spoon.
s.m. A slight shower.

n. m. Short heavy showers as in June—July.

s.f. A small boat, canoe, skiff, barge; a small trough; a small boat-shaped receptacle made of leaves (for ghī, &c.).
more: s. (dakh.) A small sort of boat.

to drink much, guzzle, tope ( = ḍukḍukānā, and ḍhoknā).

ety: obl. pl. of do; and = Prk. दोण्हं or दोण्हहुं (gen. plur.)
def: adj. The two, both, both of them
subs: adv. At the mingline of day and night, at dusk
Day and night to mingle, day to shade into night, to become dusk
lit. 'To clap with both bands'; to reciprocate, to meet half way; to give as good as one gets.

def: conj. Both; either. 1. Purakh, māl bin pās paṛausan jib na ko patyāvegā , Rus. Jab tauṅ donoṅ hāth malegī, aur jīrā pachhtāvegā . When neighbour, wanting husband, wealth, no credit you command, Then grief will overwhelm your soul as you rub hand 'gainst hand. 2. Donoṅ hāthoṅ pagṛī saṅbhālnī paṛī . Prov. He has to support his turban with both hands. (He is hard put to support his position). 3. Donoṅ ghar ābad! Donoṅ ghar chirāg jale! Benediction. Prosperity attend them both! (Said of matrimonial alliances, or of a decision which satisfies both parties). donoṅ sūrat meṅ , adj. In either case. donoṅ taraf , adj. On both sides. donoṅ vaqt milte , adv. s.f. Prayer, supplication (to God); an invocation of good, a blessing, benediction; wish; congratulation, salutation; imprecation of evil, curse, maiediction ( = bad-duʻā); — a verse, or verses, of the Qorʼān; — an incantation (pl. adʻiyat, or vulg. adʻiya)
more: n. دعو prayed. 1. Benediction; blessing. 2. Prayer; invocation; an incantation. 3. An āyat or text from the Qurān. Duā aur davā donoṅ karnī chāhiye . Prov. Prayer and medicine should go together. duā paṛh'nā , v. a. To read the praises of God in the Korān. Kaī ham-dameṅ thīṅ jo kuchh kuchh paṛhīṅ, Duāëṅ wo paṛh-paṛh-ke āge baṛhīṅ . Mīr Hasan. duā-i-khair , n. f. A prayer for one's welfare. duā-i-daulat , Prayer for one's prosperity; benediction. duā denā , v. a. To give a blessing; to bless or pray for. duā salām kahnā , v. a. To send one's compliments or blessings. duā-go , n. m. 1. One who blesses or prays; beadsman. 2. A well-wisher; a friend. duā māṅgnā, dast ba-duā honā , v. n. To pray for; bless; to lift up (one's hands in prayer). Māṅgā kareṅge ab se duā hijr-i-yār kī, Akhir to dushmanī hai asar ko duā ke sāth . Momin. bad-duā ; H. sarāp , n. m. Calling down curses on. Kisī kī bad…

Benediction, blessing.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُعَا (du'ā). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi دعا (d'a /⁠du'ā⁠/).

more: (from دو or द्वौ ) s. m. The two or deuce at cards, dice, &c.

du-ćandān, adj. Twice as much, as much again, double, two-fold

v.n. To die young, to die soon

s.m. A soil consisting of clay and sand
s.m. A mixture of two kinds of things (as of tobacco, &c.)
du-rāsta, adj. Having a row of trees, or of houses, on both sides (a road)
adj. & s.m. Capricious, whimsical; — half-bred; — a mongrel, a person of double breed (having good qualities on one side, and bad on the other, so as not to be depended on; syn. doglā)
du-rangā, adj. (f. -ī), Of two colours; piebald; — capricious; double-dealing; hypocritical; — s.m. A capricious person; a double-dealer; a hypocrite

adj. & s.m. Two-faced, double-faced, hypocritical; — a double-faced person, a hypocrite; — name of a flower (one face of which is yellow and the other red; syn. gul-ě-raʻnā)

s.f. The having two faces, double-facedness; duplicity, &c. ( = du-rangī, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Double-faced, &c. ( = do-rū); — adv. On both faces, or sides
(or do-) zānū baiṭhnā or ho-baiṭhnā, v.n. To kneel and sit on the heels behind; to sit on the hams or haunches
s.f. The being forked-tongued; the having two tongues, duplicity; equivocation
v.n. To pierce through and through, to go right through
adj. Of two years, two years old; biennial; — s.m. A two-year old colt; — lands that have been two years under cultivation
do-sāʼī, adj. & s.f. Yielding two crops a year (land; = du-fas̤lī)
s.f. The ploughing land twice; twice-ploughed land
s.m., or du-sūtī, s.f. A kind of cloth, the threads of which are double
s.m., or du-serī, s.f. A weight of two sers
adj. & s.m. Having two branches; forked (a branch); — the fork of a branch

adj. Double-bolted (as a lock); double-barrelled (as a gun); inflicting two blows, or a double wound
adj. (f. -ī), Having two sides, two-sided; on both sides, mutual, taking or pretending to take both sides in a dispute, deceitful
s.f. Double rule, the rule of two masters
adj. & s.f. Yielding two crops or harvests in the year (viz. the rabīʻ or spring harvest and the ḵẖarīf or autumnal harvest); — land yielding two crops in the year
v.t. To divide into two parts, to cut in two, to bisect
adj. Two-personed; fit for two persons
s.m. A double padlock, iron chains for the legs of criminals
adj. & s.m. Two-hunched; — a two-hunched camel, the Bactrian camel
(or do-) gāṛā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Twice, double; — a musket that carries two balls, a double-barrelled gun
adj. & s.m. Double, two together; of two sorts; — two genuflexions in prayer; a prayer in which two genuflexions are made; — two intimate friends, an inseparable pair
(or ḍo-) gun, du-gunā, adj. (f. -ī), Double, two-fold, twice as much

(or do-) ganḍī ćiṭṭī, s.f. A go-between, a fomenter of quarrels, one who flatters both parties in a dispute (the expression is taken from a game in which ćiṭṭī or tamarind seeds are used as dice, the seed being marked or rubbed on one face or side; and if it be fraudulently marked on both sides, it is called du-ganḍī)

du-maṭ, adj. & s.m. Consisting of two soils; — a mixture of two soils

du-manzila, adj. Of two floors or stories (a house, &c.); of two decks (a ship)

def: (from دو Two, and آب Water) A country between two rivers; the country (we call the Doab) between the Gangā and Jamunā. See انتربيد antarbed.
direct plural of دُعا (du'ā, “prayer, supplication”)
the Lord's Prayer

ety: after the model of A., fr. دعائيّة, fem. of دعائی
def: adj. Benedictory; — s.f. Benediction, &c. ( = duʻā)
root: د ع ا ئ ي ي

def: s. f. (dakh.) A belt. see دوالي dūʼālī.
s.f. A leather strap, a belt ( = duʼāl)
more: (properly دوال q. v.) s. f. A leather strap, a belt. دوالي بند dūʼālī-band, A sipāhī (having a leathern belt).
s. A belt of thread or string tied round the loins, to which the langoṭī is fastened ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. lit. 'Having a leather belt,' a sipāhī, or sepoy.

def: oblique plural of دُعا (du'ā, “prayer, supplication”)
n. m. The third yug or brazen age, comprising 8,64,000 years.
n. m. A door-keeper.
s. m. Benediction, invocation, prayer.
s.f. Bent grass, panic grass, Panicum dactylon, or Cynodon dactylon (commonly used as fodder for horses, &c.).
more: n. f. S. दूर्बा a grass, Pālī, dubbā . A fine soft green grass, highly esteemed for fodder; Cynodor dactylon . Wat. 1. Zamīdārī dūb kī jaṛ hai . Prov. An estate, like the roots of the dūb , is always bearing. 2. Nānuk! nannhā ho raho jaisi nannhī dūb! Aur ghās jal jāeṅgī, dūb khūb kī khūb . Nānak Shāh. Be modest Nānak! as the fresh soft dūb doth lowly lie, While other grasses scorched up are, the dūb's bloom ne'er doth die. 3. Dahī, dūrbā, rochan, phal mūlā, Nav tulsī dal, maṅgal mūlā . Rāmāyan. dūb kī nāl, lit . the sheath of dūb ; hence, Family connections, so called from their tendency to spread far and wide like dūb. kheṛe kī dūb, lit . the grass of a common. A poor man of no account.
E. n. m. S. द्रुड to sink. Dip; immersion; soaking.

more: n. f. S. द्वैधक, Pālī, dvelhakam . 1. Doubt; suspense; dilemma. 2. Ambiguity; uncertainty. 1. Apne apne manan kī sabhoṅ ne līnī mān, Satt dharam dubdhā rahī, parī na kāhū jān . Tulsī Dās. All go their own ways just as each one different thinks, The true faith none hath found, each one misdoubting shrinks. 2. Musalmān uṭh Makke jāve, Hindū Gangā nhātā hai; Dubdhā hī meṅ janam gaṅvāyā, nis din gāfil rahtā hai . Brindsatsahī. The Musalman to Mecca goes, in the Ganges Hindus dip, In doubts and heedlessness their nights and days all from them slip. dubdhā k. , v. n. To be in a state of doubt; to doubt; mistrust ( āgā pīchhā k .).
s. f. Doubt, suspense, uncertainty, dilemma.

more: adj. S. दूर्भर, Pālī. dūharo . See اجيرن 7, 9—11. 1. Chhuddar laṛikā se to jāno dūbhar ho jāle . Bhoj. A wicked child makes life a burden. 2. Ek bīr saë rakhnī dūbhar, Kaṭhan rākhnā saë dū kā! W. Rus. song. One wife to keep is hard for you, How hard 'twould be to keep wives two! 3. Tum mujhe dūbhar nahīṅ ho . You are not a burden to me. 4. Chain is bachche ke rone se nahīṅ dam bhar mujhe, Khojṛe-pīṭe ne jīnā kar dīyā dūbhar mujhe . Wom. Rāhat. This child is always crying, not a moment's peace have I, The wretch just makes me drag a life of longdrawn misery. 5. Arshad, aise ishq meṅ sadme sahe, Ishq bhī ab mujh ko dūbhar ho gayā . Arshad. Love's darts have wounded Arshad so That Love to him 's unwelcome now.
